items.text: Dana Whitfield, product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland
Sam, partner of six years; described as 'great' support during difficult times
Marcus, brother, lives in Denver; they are close but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact
Mid-quarter at work with roadmap review on Thursday 2025-02-06; working through prioritization between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for exec team
Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact — described as 'a real intention'
Lost mother recently (as of 2025-02-03 session, described as 'heavy winter'); said 'it colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in this session
Priya, closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer; they signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting 2025-02-12
Started attending Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya (as of 2025-02-12); first standing regular activity they've had together in years

Mother had a 22-foot wooden sailboat called the Dorothy Jean, named after Dana's grandmother. Mom taught Dana to sail on that boat. The boat has been under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose since mom got sick. On Sunday 2025-02-09, Dana pulled the tarp off and sanded one plank — the first step in deciding to restore the boat slowly, with no deadline. Dana said working with hands on something of mom's 'felt like the first right thing in months.' (2025-02-12)
Restoring mother's sailboat Dorothy Jean at barn near Scappoose; working Sundays, slowly, no deadline (started 2025-02-09)

Training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; signed up 2025-02-24. Running four times per week with long run every Saturday morning. Did eight miles on Saturday 2025-02-22 along the river. Has been a casual runner for years (5Ks, one half marathon about four years ago). Chose marathon as 'something with a long arc' that's just theirs during difficult winter.
On 2025-02-24, Dana said of signing up for the marathon: 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'
